content.thinking: Now let me check the Elasticsearch StatefulSet configuration to understand how the pods are currently configured.

content.text: Good, I can see the credentials in the secret. Let me check the StatefulSet configuration and current Elasticsearch settings.

input.command: kubectl get statefulset es-cluster -n elasticsearch -o yaml 2>&1

content.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"StatefulSet","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"es-cluster","namespace":"elasticsearch"},"spec":{"podManagementPolicy":"Parallel","replicas":3,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"es-cluster"}},"serviceName":"es-cluster","template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"es-cluster"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"env":[{"name":"POD_NAME","valueFrom":{"fieldRef":{"fieldPath":"metadata.name"}}},{"name":"ES_JAVA_OPTS","value":"-Xms512m -Xmx512m"}],"image":"docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1","name":"elasticsearch","ports":[{"containerPort":9200,"name":"http"},{"containerPort":9300,"name":"transport"}],"readinessProbe":{"failureThreshold":6,"httpGet":{"path":"/_cluster/health?local=true","port":"http"},"initialDelaySeconds":10,"periodSeconds":5,"timeoutSeconds":2},"resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"1","memory":"2Gi"},"requests":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"1Gi"}},"volumeMounts":[{"mountPath":"/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml","name":"es-config","subPath":"elasticsearch.yml"},{"mountPath":"/usr/share/elasticsearch/plugins","name":"es-plugins"},{"mountPath":"/mnt/secure-settings","name":"secure-settings","readOnly":true},{"mountPath":"/usr/share/elasticsearch/data","name":"data"}]}],"initContainers":[{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","set -e\nif [ ! -d /usr/share/elasticsearch/plugins/repository-s3 ]; then\n  /usr/share/elasticsearch/bin/elasticsearch-plugin install --batch repository-s3\nfi\n"],"image":"docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1","name":"install-s3-plugin","volumeMounts":[{"mountPath":"/usr/share/elasticsearch/plugins","name":"es-plugins"}]}],"volumes":[{"configMap":{"items":[{"key":"elasticsearch.yml","path":"elasticsearch.yml"}],"name":"es-config"},"name":"es-config"},{"emptyDir":{},"name":"es-plugins"},{"name":"secure-settings","secret":{"secretName":"es-secure-settings"}}]}},"updateStrategy":{"type":"OnDelete"},"volumeClaimTemplates":[{"metadata":{"name":"data"},"spec":{"accessModes":["ReadWriteOnce"],"resources":{"requests":{"storage":"5Gi"}}}}]}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-23T13:25:24Z"
  generation: 1
  name: es-cluster
  namespace: elasticsearch
  resourceVersion: "896447"
  uid: be212e47-44eb-4b69-bb5f-527c07da62d7
spec:
  persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy:
    whenDeleted: Retain
    whenScaled: Retain
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  replicas: 3
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: es-cluster
  serviceName: es-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: es-cluster
    spec:
      containers:
      - env:
        - name: POD_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
          value: -Xms512m -Xmx512m
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: elasticsearch
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9200
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 9300
          name: transport
          protocol: TCP
        readinessProbe:
          failureThreshold: 6
          httpGet:
            path: /_cluster/health?local=true
            port: http
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 2
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "1"
            memory: 2Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
          name: es-config
          subPath: elasticsearch.yml
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/plugins
          name: es-plugins
        - mountPath: /mnt/secure-settings
          name: secure-settings
          readOnly: true
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
          name: data
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      initContainers:
      - command:
        - /bin/sh
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          if [ ! -d /usr/share/elasticsearch/plugins/repository-s3 ]; then
            /usr/share/elasticsearch/bin/elasticsearch-plugin install --batch repository-s3
          fi
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: install-s3-plugin
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/plugins
          name: es-plugins
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
      volumes:
      - configMap:
          defaultMode: 420
          items:
          - key: elasticsearch.yml
            path: elasticsearch.yml
          name: es-config
        name: es-config
      - emptyDir: {}
        name: es-plugins
      - name: secure-settings
        secret:
          defaultMode: 420
          secretName: es-secure-settings
  updateStrategy:
    type: OnDelete
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - apiVersion: v1
    kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      name: data
    spec:
      accessModes:
      - ReadWriteOnce
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 5Gi
      volumeMode: Filesystem
    status:
      phase: Pending
status:
  availableReplicas: 3
  collisionCount: 0
  currentReplicas: 3
  currentRevision: es-cluster-96f876476
  observedGeneration: 1
  readyReplicas: 3
  replicas: 3
  updateRevision: es-cluster-96f876476
  updatedReplicas: 3

input.description: List and show configmaps in elasticsearch namespace

content.content:
NAME               DATA   AGE
es-config          1      3m51s
kube-root-ca.crt   1      3m53s
apiVersion: v1
items:
- apiVersion: v1
  data:
    elasticsearch.yml: |
      cluster.name: es-cluster
      node.name: ${POD_NAME}
      node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]
      network.host: 0.0.0.0
      discovery.seed_hosts:
        - es-cluster-0.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
        - es-cluster-1.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
        - es-cluster-2.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      cluster.initial_master_nodes:
        - es-cluster-0
        - es-cluster-1
        - es-cluster-2
      node.store.allow_mmap: false
      xpack.security.enabled: false
      xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: false
      xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: false
      s3.client.default.endpoint: minio.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local:9000
      s3.client.default.protocol: http
      s3.client.default.path_style_access: true

  metadata:

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture of the setup:

1. **Elasticsearch 8.11.1** running as 3-node StatefulSet
2. **MinIO** running as a Deployment
3. The `es-secure-settings` Secret contains:
   - `s3.client.default.access_key`: `minioadmin`
   - `s3.client.default.secret_key`: `minioadmin`
4. The ConfigMap has the S3 endpoint configured in `elasticsearch.yml` (but credentials shouldn't be there, they're in the keystore)
5. The StatefulSet mounts the secret at `/mnt/secure-settings` - but this is NOT the right way to load secure settings into Elasticsearch. 

The real problem is that Elasticsearch reads secure settings from its own keystore file (`/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.keystore`), not from mounted directories. To properly inject these S3 credentials, I need to either use the `elasticsearch-keystore` CLI tool to add them to each pod's keystore, or leverage the Elasticsearch Keystore API to set them dynamically. The mounted secret files won't automatically load into the keystore, so I need to use `kubectl exec` to add the credentials from those files to each pod's keystore, then trigger a reload of the secure settings across the cluster.

Writing shell commands... I need to verify the S3 plugin is installed and check the keystore status in each pod, then work through adding the credentials, reloading the secure settings, registering the snapshot repository, and finally creating a snapshot.

content.text:
The setup is clear. The secret is mounted at `/mnt/secure-settings` but Elasticsearch reads secure settings from its keystore, not from a mounted directory. I need to:
1. Add credentials to the keystore in each pod
2. Reload secure settings
3. Register the repository and create a snapshot

Let me check the current keystore state and plugin installation:


So the keystore already has the S3 credentials! And the mounted secret files are at `/mnt/secure-settings/`.

Wait, but then why are snapshots failing? Let me check what plugins are installed. The output only showed the keystore list, not the plugins directory. Let me check again.
Actually, looking more carefully at the output - the plugins directory listing was probably empty (no output), and what I'm seeing is just the keystore entries. So the S3 credentials are already configured in the keystore, but I need to check the init container to see if there's something else going on with the snapshot configuration.

The init container in the StatefulSet installs the repository-s3 plugin, and since the volume is emptyDir, each pod restart requires reinstalling it. Let me verify the plugins directory and check the cluster health to see if everything's properly configured.
